2. Use Collapsible from shadcn/ui (Radix) for expand/collapse with ChevronRight/ChevronDown icons
3. Persist expanded/collapsed state in app-store.ts (or localStorage) so groups stay consistent across navigation
4. When sidebar is collapsed (icon-only mode), show all items flat (groups don't apply in collapsed mode)
5. Add subtle group header styling - muted text, smaller font, with toggle affordance
6. Smooth transition on expand/collapse (150ms per spec animation guidelines)

Files to modify: frontend/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx, frontend/src/stores/app-store.ts (add navGroupState)
May need: frontend/src/components/ui/collapsible.tsx (if not already present, install from shadcn)

title: fix right drawer header alignment with page section headers
description:
Right drawer header uses 'px-4 py-3 border-b' while the projects page header uses 'px-6 py-4 border-b'. The horizontal lines don't align across the right drawer and the main content area. Harmonize the padding/height so the drawer header border-b aligns with the page section header border-b.

Files: frontend/src/components/layout/right-drawer.tsx (header div), relevant page headers

title: fix left sidebar collapse icon vertical alignment with bottom log panel
description:
The left sidebar collapse/expand icon sits too high relative to the bottom logs panel. The sidebar footer uses 'border-t border-border p-2 shrink-0' while the logs component uses 'flex items-center h-9 px-4 gap-2'. Decrease the sidebar footer component height so its border-t aligns with the logs panel border-t line. The collapse icon should be vertically centered and aligned with the Logs label row.

Files: frontend/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx (footer div), frontend/src/components/layout/bottom-panel.tsx

title: fix AI suggestions empty state text touching sidebar border
description:
On the AI Suggestions page, the 'No suggestions yet. Click Generate to analyse the current run.' text touches the left sidebar border. Needs proper left padding/margin so text is positioned within the content area with appropriate spacing.

Files: frontend/src/pages/suggestions-page.tsx

title: remove excess borders around filter dropdowns on suggestions and artifacts pages
description:
On the AI Suggestions page, the 'All statuses' dropdown has a top and bottom border line that creates too many horizontal lines. Same issue on the Artifacts page for both 'All types' and 'All runs' dropdowns — there are top and bottom borders wrapping those filter dropdowns that add visual clutter. Remove the surrounding border-t/border-b on the filter bar containers.

Files: frontend/src/pages/suggestions-page.tsx, frontend/src/pages/artifacts-page.tsx

title: make save settings button sticky at bottom-right and fix retention card spacing
description:
Two issues on the Settings page:

1. Save Settings button should be persistent/sticky at the bottom-right corner of the viewport so users don't have to scroll to the bottom to save. Currently it sits inline between the Watchdog and Default Checkpoint Retention cards.

2. The Default Checkpoint Retention and Experiment Retention cards have no gap between them and don't match the padding/spacing of the upper cards (AI Provider, Storage, Watchdog). Align all settings cards to use consistent padding, gap, and formatting.

Files: frontend/src/pages/settings-page.tsx, frontend/src/components/settings/settings-form.tsx, frontend/src/components/settings/default-retention-policy.tsx

title: fix transformers not installed error when resolving huggingface model
description:
When resolving a HuggingFace model, the backend raises 'transformers is not installed' because transformers is in [project.optional-dependencies] training extras, not base dependencies. Same issue exists for datasets library in dataset_service.py.

Root cause: pyproject.toml line 30-38 puts transformers and datasets in optional 'training' group. But model_service.py:44 and dataset_service.py:164 need them for core resolve operations (not just training).

Fix: Move 'transformers>=4.40.0' and 'datasets>=2.0.0' from [project.optional-dependencies].training to [project].dependencies. Keep torch, peft, trl, bitsandbytes in the training extras since those are truly training-only.

Files: backend/pyproject.toml (move deps), backend/app/services/model_service.py (can simplify import guard), backend/app/services/dataset_service.py (can simplify import guard)

title: add structural flow visualization tab to weights & architecture page
description:
Add a new 'Flow' tab to the Weights & Architecture page (alongside Architecture, Parameters, Activations, Deltas, Expert Edit) that renders the model as a horizontal flow diagram derived from the existing LayerNode architecture tree.

## Layout
- Horizontal scroll canvas. Each column is a major layer group. Left-to-right order follows the tree depth-first traversal of top-level children of the root node.
- Typical columns for a transformer: Embedding → [Transformer Block] × N → LM Head (or equivalent for non-causal models)
- Within each column, rows are the sub-components of that group (e.g. for a transformer block: LayerNorm, Self-Attention subdivided into Q/K/V projections, MLP sublayers)
- Curved SVG connector lines flow from each column's output to the next column's input, visually encoding the data path

## Repeated Block Collapsing
- Detect consecutive identical blocks (same name pattern, same child structure) — e.g. Llama's 32 decoder layers
- Collapse them into a single column card with a badge showing 'N×' (e.g. '32× TransformerBlock')
- Expand/collapse toggle on the card to show all individual block columns or the collapsed summary
- Collapsed state is default

## Interaction
- Click any node/row within a column to open the existing right drawer layer-detail panel (reuse openLayerDetailDrawer from app-store)
- Hover tooltip showing: layer name, type, param count, shape, trainable status
- Column header shows the group name and total param count for the group
- Horizontal scroll with mouse wheel or trackpad

## Implementation
- Pure frontend — derives graph from existing ModelArchitectureResponse (tree: LayerNode) from useModelArchitecture hook
- No new backend endpoints required
- Build a graph derivation utility: flattenToFlowColumns({ tree: LayerNode }) -> FlowColumn[] where FlowColumn has { label, nodes: FlowNode[], isRepeated, repeatCount }
- Render using SVG (inline, no external graph library needed — recharts is available but SVG paths are sufficient for the connector lines)
- New component: frontend/src/components/weights/flow-visualization.tsx
- New types in frontend/src/types/model.ts or a separate flow-types.ts: FlowColumn, FlowNode, FlowEdge
- Wired into weights-page.tsx as a new TabsTrigger value='flow' / TabsContent

## Design
- Match the app's muted color palette — column cards use Card component, connector lines use stroke='hsl(var(--muted-foreground))' with opacity
- Trainable layers highlighted with a subtle accent color border
- Frozen layers muted
- Consistent with spec §26 UI design direction (clean, low noise, expert-friendly)

title: add activation-backed token flow mode to architecture flow visualization
description:
Extends llm-interactive-1727 (structural flow visualization). Adds a second view mode within the Flow tab that overlays real activation data on top of the structural graph, turning rows into actual input tokens and encoding activation magnitudes as visual weight on the connector lines.

## Depends on
llm-interactive-1727 must be complete first — this builds on top of the FlowVisualization component and FlowColumn types it introduces.

## What changes
Add a mode toggle to the Flow tab: 'Structural' (default, from 1727) | 'Activation'

### Activation Mode
- User enters a sample text prompt in an input field (reuses ActivationSampleSelector pattern)
- On capture, calls the existing POST /projects/{id}/models/activations endpoint with all visible layer names
- Rows become individual input tokens extracted from the sample (tokenized display, not raw IDs — show decoded token strings)
- Each row×column cell shows a colored intensity tile based on the layer's activation mean for that token position
- Connector line thickness and opacity between columns encodes the mean activation magnitude flowing from the source layer to the destination layer
- Color scale: low activation = muted blue, high activation = accent purple (matching app color palette)
- Hovering a cell shows: token string, layer name, activation mean, std, min, max (from TierOneStats)

### Data mapping
- Existing ActivationSnapshotResponse returns per-layer TierOneStats (mean, std, min, max, histogram_bins) and shape
- Shape encodes [batch, seq_len, hidden_dim] — use seq_len dimension to map token positions to rows
- Token strings: derive from the sample input split on whitespace/subwords for display (exact tokenization not required for v1 — word-level splitting is acceptable)

### Interaction
- Captured snapshots persist in the same capturedSnapshots state already used by the Activations tab
- Snapshot selector dropdown if multiple captures exist (reuse compareIndex pattern from activations tab)
- 'Capture' button triggers activation capture for all currently visible layer names in the flow graph

## Implementation notes
- Extend FlowVisualization component props with: mode, activationSnapshot, onCaptureRequest
- Add ActivationOverlay sub-component within flow-visualization.tsx or as a sibling
- No new backend endpoints — uses existing activation capture + snapshot fetch hooks
- New frontend types: FlowActivationCell, TokenRow

title: training screens show blank when no project selected — auto-carry selected project from dashboard
description: The Training, Adapters & Organizations, and Weights & Architecture screens show blank content with no way to select a project. If a project is already selected in the dashboard, it should automatically be treated as the active project on these screens. The fix should: (1) propagate the dashboard-selected project into the training-related screens via shared store or context, and (2) show a prompt to select a project if none is selected, rather than a blank screen. Acceptance: all three screens show content or a clear CTA when visited after selecting a project on the dashboard, and do not show blank when a project is already active.

title: architecture layer click returns 404 for layer detail endpoint
description: Clicking a layer in the Architecture tab triggers GET /api/v1/projects/{id}/models/layers/{layer_name} which returns 404. Example: GET /api/v1/projects/5b714ed1-0364-45b5-b8d7-be51fc038e22/models/layers/Qwen2ForCausalLM.lm_head 404. The backend route for fetching individual layer details either does not exist or the URL pattern does not match. Investigate the backend route registration for layer detail, check what endpoint the frontend is calling, and either implement the missing route/service method or correct the URL to match an existing endpoint. Acceptance: clicking a layer in the Architecture tab loads layer detail without errors.

title: training and adapters&optimization pages still blank — previous fix ineffective
description: After the previous fix (persist activeProjectId in store, add NoProjectSelected component), the Training and Adapters & Optimization pages still show blank with no content and no empty-state CTA. No additional console errors are shown. The fix either did not wire the store correctly, the store key name differs from what the pages read, or the pages are not re-rendering when the store value changes. Investigate: (1) what key the store uses for the selected project, (2) what the pages read to determine the active project, (3) whether the component renders but shows blank due to data-fetch failure vs truly unmounted/empty. Fix root cause. Acceptance: both pages show NoProjectSelected CTA when no project is active, and show page content when a project is selected on the dashboard.

title: adapters & optimization screen crashes on open with TypeError reading undefined.includes
description: Navigating to Adapters & Optimization causes a full crash/blank screen with: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'includes') at FSe (minified component). The stack points to an Array.map call in a component that calls .includes() on a value that is undefined — likely a config field (e.g. adapter type list, optimization method list) that is undefined when the config hasn't loaded yet or is missing from the active config. Fix with a null-guard before the .includes() call and ensure the component renders gracefully when config data is not yet available. Acceptance: Adapters & Optimization screen loads without crashing regardless of config state.

title: log output panel should be collapsed by default
description: The log output panel/section in the run detail or training view should start in a collapsed state by default. Users should be able to expand it. Currently it is expanded by default which takes up space. Find the component that renders the log output (likely in components/runs/ or the right drawer panel) and set its initial expanded state to false/collapsed.

title: training page config save should show a success notification
description: After saving config on the Training page, there is no visual feedback. A toast or inline notification should appear confirming the save succeeded (and an error notification if it failed). Use whatever toast/notification system is already established in the codebase (check components/ui/ for existing toast usage). Acceptance: saving config on training page shows a success toast; failure shows an error toast.

title: runs screen should allow initiating runs directly — remove training-page dependency
description: The Runs page currently tells users to go to the Training screen to start a run. This is wrong UX — Training is for config management, Runs is where you launch and monitor runs. The Runs page should have a primary action to create/initiate a new run directly (e.g. a 'New Run' or 'Start Run' button). This requires: (1) a way to select or confirm the active config version to use, (2) a call to the run creation API, (3) the new run appearing in the runs list. Remove any CTA that redirects to Training. Acceptance: users can start a run from the Runs screen without visiting the Training screen.

title: weights & architecture flow view should show detailed layer breakdown like architecture view
description: The Flow view in the Weights & Architecture screen should be as detailed as the Architecture tab — showing decoder layers, and within each: self-attention (with q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj), MLP (with gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj), layer norm, etc. Currently it is a simplified/high-level graph. The goal is a flow-graph visualizer version of the architecture tree: same nodes, but rendered as a connected flow diagram rather than a tree list. Leverage the existing architecture data (same API/hooks that power the architecture view) and render it as a detailed flow graph. Acceptance: the Flow view in Weights & Architecture shows the same layer granularity as the Architecture view, with self-attention submodules and MLP submodules visible as connected nodes.

title: flow view: replace horizontal scroll with freeform canvas, fix layout and arrows
description: The current flow view has multiple problems visible in the screenshot: nodes overflow their bounds, arrows are misaligned/truncated, and the layout is a horizontal scroll. Replace the entire layout approach: (1) Convert the flow canvas to a freeform pan-and-drag canvas (no horizontal/vertical scroll — infinite canvas, drag to pan, similar to Figma/Excalidraw interaction model). Use a transform/translate approach on a container div, with mousedown+mousemove drag handling. (2) Fix node sizing so content never overflows bounds — nodes must size to their content. (3) Fix arrow/edge routing so arrows connect node centers or ports cleanly and do not float in empty space. (4) Lay out nodes in a proper grid (columns × rows) so the initial view is well-organized without manual panning. The implementation should NOT require a heavy library like React Flow — use the existing SVG overlay + positioned div approach already in place, but fix the math. Acceptance: flow view starts in a clean grid layout, nodes don't overflow, arrows connect correctly, user can pan by dragging the canvas.

title: move save config to persistent bottom-right bar on training and adapters pages, remove duplicate start run button
description: Three UI fixes: (1) Training page: move the Save Config button out of the top header/form area into a fixed bottom bar (fixed bottom-right, same pattern as settings-sticky-save-button in mulch). (2) Adapters & Optimization page: same — move Save Config button to a fixed bottom-right bar. (3) Runs page: remove the duplicate 'Start Run' button in the empty-state content area (center of page). Keep only the one in the top-right header. The empty state should still show a 'No runs yet' message but without a button. Reference the existing settings page implementation for the sticky save bar pattern.

title: add training presets panel and adapters presets panel with baseline defaults
description: Both the Training page and Adapters & Optimization page have empty space on the right side of the content area. Use that space for a presets/defaults sidebar panel: (1) Training page: a right-side panel (or collapsible aside) showing preset configurations for training parameters (optimization settings, interval settings, training loop — e.g. 'Fast convergence', 'Memory efficient', 'Stable baseline'). Clicking a preset populates the relevant form fields with sensible defaults. (2) Adapters & Optimization page: same concept — preset cards for common adapter configurations (e.g. 'LoRA minimal', 'QLoRA 4-bit', 'Full LoRA'). Clicking a preset fills in the adapter form fields. Presets should be hardcoded constants (no API needed). The panel should sit to the right of the main form content in the page layout, not as a drawer. Acceptance: both pages show a presets panel; clicking a preset populates the form fields with the preset values.

title: add save toast to adapters and settings pages, set toast auto-dismiss to 2s
description: Training page already shows a toast on config save. Adapters & Optimization page and Settings page do not. Fix: (1) wire success/error toast to the save action on the adapters page, (2) wire success/error toast to the save action on the settings page. Additionally, the toast duration is too long — change the auto-dismiss duration to 2000ms (2 seconds) for all toasts. Find where the toast duration is configured (likely in use-toast.ts or the toast call sites) and set it to 2000.

title: flow tab structural view blank after canvas overhaul
description: After the recent flow canvas overhaul, the Structural view in the Flow tab on the Weights & Architecture page shows completely blank — no nodes, no edges, no loading state. The Activation tab toggle is visible but the canvas area is empty. Root cause is likely: the data is not being passed into the new flow-visualization.tsx correctly, the component is not rendering because a required prop is undefined or falsy, or the canvas layout calculation produces zero-size output. Investigate: (1) what data source the Structural mode uses (architecture hook vs a dedicated flow hook), (2) whether that data is defined when the component renders, (3) whether the new pan canvas or ResizeObserver setup has a timing/mount issue that prevents initial render. Fix so the Structural flow view renders the model nodes on load.

title: add copy-for-ai button to models, training, adapters, and weights pages
description:
Add a subtle 'Copy for AI' utility button to the header bar of four pages. When clicked, it copies a self-contained prompt (system prompt + structured JSON data) to the clipboard that the user can paste directly into Claude, GPT, or any AI assistant.

SHARED COMPONENT: frontend/src/components/shared/copy-for-ai.tsx
- Props: { buildPrompt: () => string, disabled?: boolean }
- Small icon button (clipboard icon, check icon on copied state for 1.5s)
- On click: calls buildPrompt(), writes to clipboard, shows 2s toast 'Copied for AI'
- Uses existing useToast hook

PROMPT BUILDER: frontend/src/lib/ai-copy-prompts.ts
- Four pure functions, each returning a formatted string: [SYSTEM PROMPT]\n...\n\n[CONTEXT]\n{ ... }
- buildModelPrompt({ profile: ModelProfile, architecture: ModelArchitectureResponse }): string — system prompt: 'You are an LLM model advisor. The user has loaded a model into their fine-tuning workbench. Help them understand what this model is — its architecture family, parameter count, memory and disk requirements, context length, vocabulary size, dtype, and what tasks it is suited for. Explain each field in plain terms and highlight anything notable about this specific model.' Data: full ModelProfile + architecture tree capped at 3 levels deep.
- buildTrainingPrompt({ training: TrainingConfig, optimization: OptimizationConfig }): string — system prompt: 'You are an LLM fine-tuning configuration advisor. The user is setting up a supervised fine-tuning run. Help them understand what each setting controls, whether their values are sensible for a typical SFT run, and flag anything that might cause instability, slow training, or poor convergence.' Data: full TrainingConfig + OptimizationConfig (all fields, no truncation).
- buildAdaptersPrompt({ adapters: AdaptersConfig, optimization: OptimizationConfig, quantization: QuantizationConfig }): string — system prompt: 'You are an LLM adapter and quantization expert. The user is configuring parameter-efficient fine-tuning with LoRA/QLoRA along with optimizer and quantization settings. Explain what each setting does, whether the combination is coherent and efficient, and suggest any adjustments to improve training quality.' Data: full AdaptersConfig + OptimizationConfig + QuantizationConfig.
- buildArchitecturePrompt({ architecture: ModelArchitectureResponse }): string — system prompt: 'You are an LLM architecture expert. The user is inspecting the internal structure of a model in their fine-tuning workbench. Below is the layer tree showing module names, types, parameter counts, trainability, and dtypes down to the projection layer level. Help them understand the structure, what each module type does, which parameters are trainable vs frozen, and how the architecture relates to the model capabilities.' Data: architecture tree capped at 3 levels deep (root → top-level modules → decoder children → projection leaves).

BUTTON PLACEMENT (all in the h-14 px-6 border-b header, right-aligned):
- models-page.tsx: render CopyForAI when profile is defined, passing buildModelPrompt({ profile, architecture })
- training-page.tsx: render CopyForAI when localTraining is non-null, passing buildTrainingPrompt({ training: localTraining, optimization: localOptimization ?? parsedConfig.optimization })
- adapters-page.tsx: render CopyForAI when localAdapters is non-null, passing buildAdaptersPrompt({ adapters: localAdapters, optimization: localOptimization, quantization: localQuantization })
- weights-page.tsx: render CopyForAI when architecture is defined, passing buildArchitecturePrompt({ architecture })

Acceptance: button appears in all four page headers when data is loaded; clicking copies a well-formatted prompt; toast confirms; pasting into Claude/GPT gives a coherent AI-ready context block.

title: run stuck at pending 0% — no logs, training process never starts
description: Run 2af85a0f-b9f9-452a-91b2-17102a496339 (project 5b714ed1-0364-45b5-b8d7-be51fc038e22) was created and shows as pending indefinitely. Progress stays at 0%. No log lines appear in the log output panel. The frontend polls the runs endpoint every ~10s and sees the run but it never transitions out of pending. The checkpoints endpoint returns 404 (expected — no checkpoints if training never started, but confirms nothing ran). Root cause is likely one of: (1) the run was inserted into the DB but the background worker/orchestrator was never triggered or failed silently on startup, (2) the orchestrator picked up the run but crashed before emitting any logs or status transitions, (3) the WebSocket log stream is not connecting to the correct run channel. Debug approach: (a) check backend logs for any exception when the run was created or when the orchestrator tried to start it, (b) check the run record in SQLite — what is its current status field and started_at, (c) check if the orchestrator service has a queue/trigger mechanism and whether it fired, (d) check WebSocket subscription on the frontend to confirm it is subscribing to the correct run ID channel. Fix the root cause so that starting a run from the Runs page actually launches the training process and emits logs.

title: weights & architecture page stuck loading model architecture
description: The Weights & Architecture page shows a loading state for model architecture that never resolves or takes excessively long. This was not previously slow. Investigate: (1) what endpoint the architecture hook calls (GET /api/v1/projects/{id}/models/architecture or similar) and whether it returns at all — check for a timeout, hanging promise, or silent error being swallowed, (2) whether the backend service that builds the architecture tree (introspection service) is doing something expensive on every request that it previously cached or skipped, (3) whether the recent layer-404 fix (which added a backendLayerName memo in weights-page.tsx) introduced any effect that re-triggers the architecture fetch on every render, (4) whether the useModelArchitecture hook has a stale query key or is re-fetching unnecessarily. Fix so architecture loads promptly on first visit as it previously did.

title: weights & architecture page: show animated loading state during slow initial architecture fetch
description: The model architecture fetch can take several seconds on initial load (model introspection). Currently the page either shows blank or a static text 'loading' state that looks like a crash. Replace with a clear animated loading state: a spinner (use the existing spinner/loading component if one exists in components/ui/, otherwise a simple Tailwind animate-spin border circle) plus a short message like 'Loading model architecture...' or 'Initializing...' centered in the content area. The loading state should be visible on the Architecture, Parameters, and Flow tabs — anywhere that depends on the architecture data. Show it while useModelArchitecture isLoading is true. Acceptance: visiting Weights & Architecture while architecture is loading shows an animated spinner with a label, making it clear the page is working and not frozen.

title: trainer process exits with non-zero status — run immediately fails
description: Run 10e7ca18-c06d-4ba8-b3f4-aa812b60e4db (project eb9af749-e4aa-414b-b865-28c38c0a4e58) fails immediately with 'Trainer process exited with non-zero status'. The trainer subprocess is crashing on startup before any training steps complete. Investigate: (1) check backend logs / stderr capture from the trainer subprocess — what is the actual Python exception or error message from the trainer process, (2) check how the orchestrator launches the trainer subprocess (subprocess.Popen or asyncio.create_subprocess_exec) and whether it captures stdout/stderr, (3) check if the trainer process is missing a required dependency, environment variable, or file path (model weights, dataset, config YAML), (4) check if the config passed to the trainer has any invalid or missing fields that cause an immediate crash during config_validation or environment_validation stage, (5) check if there is a GPU/MPS device issue — the trainer may be trying to use a device that is unavailable. Fix so the trainer process starts successfully and progresses through stages.

title: checkpoints endpoint returns 404
description: GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/runs/{run_id}/checkpoints returns 404. The route either does not exist in the backend or the URL pattern does not match. Investigate: (1) search backend/app/api/routes/ for a checkpoints route handler, (2) check if it is registered in main.py or the runs router, (3) if the route exists but returns 404, check whether it queries by run_id correctly and returns an empty list (not 404) when no checkpoints exist yet. Fix: the endpoint should return 200 with an empty array when the run exists but has no checkpoints, and 404 only when the run itself does not exist.

title: trainer receives empty model_id at model resolution stage — run fails immediately
description: Trainer crashes at model_resolution stage with HFValidationError: repo id is empty string ''. The model_id field in the config passed to the trainer subprocess is empty or missing. Root cause is a config field mapping mismatch: the WorkbenchConfig YAML uses camelCase (modelId) but the trainer likely reads snake_case (model_id), OR the model section of the config is not being written to the YAML correctly when the run is created, OR the trainer is reading from the wrong key. Investigate: (1) backend/app/services/trainer.py line 355 — what key does _stage_model_resolution read for the model id? (2) the YAML config that gets written to disk before the trainer subprocess is launched — does it contain a non-empty model.model_id field? (3) backend/app/services/orchestrator.py — how does it pass the config to the trainer? Does it write the YAML and pass the path, or pass fields directly? (4) check if the WorkbenchConfig serialization to YAML converts camelCase keys to snake_case — if not, the trainer reading model_id gets empty string while the YAML has modelId. Fix the key mismatch so the trainer receives the correct non-empty model id.

title: trainer fails at dataset resolution with empty dataset_id — IndexError list index out of range
description: Trainer crashes at dataset_resolution stage. Traceback: load_dataset(dataset_id, subset) receives an empty string for dataset_id, causing path.split('/') to produce an empty list and [-1] to throw IndexError. Same class of bug as the model_id fix — the dataset_id field is not being persisted to the config YAML or is not being read correctly. Investigate: (1) trainer.py _stage_dataset_resolution — add an explicit guard: if not dataset_id: raise a clear ConfigurationError('dataset_id is empty — configure a dataset before starting a run') so the error message is actionable instead of a cryptic IndexError. (2) Investigate whether dataset_id suffers the same persistence gap as model_id — the dataset configuration in the frontend may write to the YAML with a different key or may not be written back at all. Check the config YAML on disk for an active project that has a dataset configured — does dataset.dataset_id (or datasetId) have a value? (3) Check if the camelCase normalization fix in trainer.py covers dataset keys (datasetId → dataset_id). If the normalization is working, the field should be populated — check whether the frontend is actually writing the dataset config to the YAML when a dataset is selected. Fix both: the guard for a clear error message, and the root cause if dataset_id is genuinely not being persisted.

title: trainer crashes at tokenization preprocessing — empty input field causes IndexError in tokenizer
description: Trainer crashes at tokenization_preprocessing stage. The _tokenize function passes an empty or None string to the tokenizer, which returns an empty tokens_and_encodings list, causing IndexError on tokens_and_encodings[0][0] inside transformers _encode_plus. Root cause: the dataset config's input_field (or inputField) key does not match an actual column name in the loaded dataset, so the text being tokenized is empty/None. Investigate: (1) trainer.py _tokenize function — what field name does it use to extract text from each example? Is it reading raw_config['dataset']['input_field'] or input_field after camelCase normalization? (2) Check what columns the loaded HuggingFace dataset actually has (dataset.column_names) vs what input_field specifies — they likely don't match. (3) Add a validation step in _stage_tokenization_preprocessing that checks the input_field column exists in the dataset before calling .map() — raise a clear ConfigurationError naming the missing column and listing available columns. (4) Check if input_field / target_field are being written to the config YAML correctly from the frontend datasets page (same write-back gap as model_id and dataset_id). Fix: add column existence validation with a clear error, and fix the field mapping if input_field is empty or wrong.

title: SFTConfig receives unexpected keyword argument max_seq_length — trl API mismatch
description: Trainer crashes at training_preparation stage with: TypeError: SFTConfig.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'max_seq_length'. This is a trl library version compatibility issue — in newer versions of trl, SFTConfig no longer accepts max_seq_length as a constructor argument (it was moved or renamed). Investigate: (1) check the installed trl version in the Docker container (pip show trl), (2) check trl SFTConfig API for the installed version — the correct argument may now be max_length, or max_seq_length may need to be passed to the SFTTrainer constructor instead of SFTConfig, or it may have been removed entirely in favor of tokenizer/DataCollator-level truncation, (3) check all kwargs being passed to SFTConfig in trainer.py and validate each against the installed trl version's API — there may be other renamed/removed arguments beyond max_seq_length, (4) fix by passing the correct argument name for the installed version, or by removing it and applying truncation at the tokenization stage instead. Acceptance: SFTConfig initializes without TypeError and training_preparation stage succeeds.

title: trainer crashes when bf16 configured but GPU unavailable — should auto-detect device and mixed precision
description: Trainer fails at training_preparation with: ValueError: Your setup doesn't support bf16/gpu. The config has mixed_precision=bf16 but the runtime has no GPU (running on CPU or MPS). The trainer should auto-detect the available device and fall back gracefully: (1) if mixed_precision is bf16 or fp16 but no CUDA GPU is available, downgrade to 'no' mixed precision automatically, (2) if device is auto, resolve it at runtime: prefer CUDA if available, then MPS (Apple Silicon), then CPU, (3) set use_cpu=True in SFTConfig when running on CPU so transformers does not reject the config, (4) add this logic in _stage_training_preparation before constructing SFTConfig — detect torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.backends.mps.is_available(), and adjust mixed_precision and device accordingly. Also apply the same detection to the execution.device config field — if set to 'auto', resolve to 'cuda'/'mps'/'cpu' at runtime. Log a warning when a fallback occurs (e.g. 'bf16 requested but no GPU available, falling back to no mixed precision'). Acceptance: trainer starts successfully on CPU-only environments with any mixed_precision setting in config.

title: SFTTrainer rejects tokenizer kwarg — trl API change
description: Trainer crashes at training_preparation with: TypeError: SFTTrainer.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'tokenizer'. In trl >= 0.9.0, SFTTrainer no longer accepts tokenizer as a direct argument — it was replaced by processing_class. Investigate: (1) check installed trl version in backend/requirements.txt or pyproject.toml, (2) find the SFTTrainer(...) constructor call in trainer.py _stage_training_preparation around line 657, (3) audit ALL kwargs passed to SFTTrainer against the installed version's actual __init__ signature by reading the trl source in site-packages — there may be multiple renamed/removed args beyond tokenizer, (4) fix: replace tokenizer= with processing_class= if trl >= 0.9, or conditionally pass the right kwarg based on the installed version. Also check dataset_text_field, packing, and any other args that may have moved between SFTConfig and SFTTrainer in recent trl versions. Do a full audit — not just the one failing kwarg — so we stop fixing one arg at a time.

title: training loop emits no progress, logs, or metrics during active run
description: Training runs successfully (CPU 100%, all prep stages green, stage=training_start) but the frontend shows 0% progress, no log entries, and metrics stuck at 'waiting for data'. The training loop in trainer.py is not emitting step-level events back to the orchestrator/frontend during training. Investigate the full observability pipeline: (1) trainer.py — does the SFTTrainer have a custom callback registered that emits step progress events (current_step, total_steps, loss, learning_rate) and log lines? If not, this is the gap — add a TrainingProgressCallback that extends transformers.TrainerCallback and calls on_log/on_step_end to publish events, (2) how does the trainer subprocess communicate back to the orchestrator — is it via a shared queue, a file, stdout JSON lines, or a WebSocket? Find the IPC mechanism and check if it is wired during the training loop (it may only be wired for stage transitions not step events), (3) backend/app/services/orchestrator.py — how does it receive and broadcast trainer events to WebSocket clients? Does it handle step_progress and log event types from the trainer? (4) frontend WebSocket hook — is it subscribed to the correct run_id channel and handling step_progress/log message types? Check frontend/src/ws/ and frontend/src/hooks/ for the run log/metrics subscription. Fix the full pipeline: trainer emits step events → orchestrator broadcasts → frontend receives and updates progress bar, logs panel, and metrics charts.

title: runs page missing historical metrics and logs fetch — only shows live WS data since page open
description: The runs page has no REST hooks for fetching historical metrics or logs from the DB. It only displays data received via WebSocket since the page was opened. This means: opening the page mid-run shows no prior progress, returning to a run after navigating away loses all history, and failed/completed runs show empty logs and metrics. Fix: (1) add useRunLogs hook that GETs /api/v1/projects/{id}/runs/{run_id}/logs from the backend (paginated or full), returning historical LogEntry rows from DB, (2) add useRunMetrics hook that GETs /api/v1/projects/{id}/runs/{run_id}/metrics, returning historical metric data points from DB, (3) in the run detail view (Logs tab and Metrics tab), seed the display with data from these hooks, then append/merge incoming live WebSocket events on top — so the view is always complete regardless of when the page was opened. Also check whether these REST endpoints exist on the backend — if not, implement them alongside the frontend hooks. Acceptance: opening the runs page for any run (active, completed, or failed) shows all historical logs and metrics from the DB, with live data appending for active runs.

title: fix app-store migrate function type error breaking docker build
description: The migrate callback in frontend/src/stores/app-store.ts line 166 casts persisted as Partial<AppState>, making all fields optional. The return type must match the partialize shape (activeProjectId, navGroupExpanded, isSidebarCollapsed, modelForm, datasetForm — all required). Fix: define a PersistedAppState type matching the partialize output shape, cast to that instead of Partial<AppState>. Both branches (version < 2 and else) must return the correct type.
closeReason: defined PersistedAppState interface matching partialize output, fixed migrate type cast from Partial<AppState> to PersistedAppState with explicit return type

title: dataset re-resolve with percentage sample returns stale split_counts, train shows 1000%
description:
Bug: On re-resolve with max_samples (percentage mode), split_counts retains the original full dataset counts instead of being recalculated after the row cap is applied.

Root cause in backend/app/services/dataset_service.py resolve_dataset() lines 298-330:
1. _load_huggingface returns split_counts with train=51760 (full HF split size)
2. rows gets capped to max_samples on line 316-317 (e.g. rows = rows[:5176] for 10%)
3. split_counts is NEVER updated after the cap — still says train=51760
4. Profile gets total_rows=len(rows)=5176 but split_counts.train=51760

Frontend impact (datasets-page.tsx line 72): trainRatio = Math.round((51760/5176)*100) = 1000%. Validation/test show null because HF only has train split.

Fix: After capping rows to max_samples, recalculate split_counts based on the user-provided train_ratio/val_ratio/test_ratio and the capped row count. If ratios are provided, compute split_counts as: train=round(total*train_ratio), validation=round(total*val_ratio), test=round(total*test_ratio). If no ratios provided, set split_counts.train=len(rows) (all rows go to train).

Also fix _write_resolved_dataset_to_config lines 266-271 which compute ratios from the stale split_counts, creating a circular error in the persisted YAML config.

File area: backend/app/services/dataset_service.py, frontend/src/pages/datasets-page.tsx
Acceptance: Re-resolve with 10% sample and 60/30/10 split shows correct ratios (60/30/10) and correct split counts (3106/1553/517 for 5176 total rows).
closeReason: fixed stale split_counts after max_samples cap in resolve_dataset — recalculates from capped total and user-provided ratios

title: default split ratios to 80/10/10 for train/validation/test
description: Currently trainRatio, valRatio, testRatio all default to null in the dataset form (frontend/src/stores/app-store.ts DEFAULT_DATASET_FORM, lines 107-109). Users must manually set ratios every time. Default to the standard ML split: trainRatio=80, valRatio=10, testRatio=10. Update DEFAULT_DATASET_FORM and bump the persist version to 3 with a migration that sets the new defaults for existing persisted stores.
closeReason: defaulted split ratios to 80/10/10 in DEFAULT_DATASET_FORM, bumped persist version to 3, added migration for existing stores

title: fix layer detail lookup failing for nested layers in flow view
description:
Bug: Clicking any nested layer in the flow view (embed_tokens, layers, norm, q_proj, gate_proj, etc.) shows loading then closes. Only top-level nodes like lm_head work.

Root cause in backend/app/services/model_service.py _find_node_in_tree() (line 226-235):
The function searches by node.name (e.g. 'embedding', 'q_proj') but the target parameter is the full dotted path from the frontend (e.g. 'model.embed_tokens.embedding', 'model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj'). Since node.name never equals the full path, the search returns None and raises LayerNotFoundError, causing the frontend dialog to show loading then close.

lm_head works because it is a direct child of the root — its node.name matches the stripped path 'lm_head'.

Fix: Rewrite _find_node_in_tree to track the accumulated path during DFS and compare the full constructed path against the target. The path should be built as parent.child notation matching PyTorch named_modules() convention.

When the model adapter is loaded (line 240-255 path), it uses named_modules() which returns full dotted paths correctly, so that path already works. Only the cached tree fallback is broken.

File area: backend/app/services/model_service.py
Acceptance: Clicking any node in the flow view (embed_tokens.embedding, layers.0.self_attn.q_proj, etc.) opens the detail dialog with correct type, params, dtype, shape info.
closeReason: fixed _find_node_in_tree to track accumulated dotted path during DFS, matching PyTorch named_modules() convention so nested layer lookups resolve correctly

title: run progress bar stuck at 0% during training — no intermediate progress between steps
description:
Bug: Active run f517ccdc shows 0% progress after 10+ minutes in training_progress stage. The progress bar never updates. Dataset profile shows total=5176, train=51760 (stale split_counts bug — separate fix merged). Duration shows '—'.

Two root causes identified:

1. BACKEND: Progress events only emit on on_step_end callback (backend/app/services/trainer.py line 248-260). With large models on CPU (Docker has no GPU), a single training step can take 10+ minutes. No progress events are emitted until the first step completes, leaving the UI at 0% indefinitely.

Fix: Add a periodic progress heartbeat in the WorkbenchCallback. During on_train_begin, record the start time. Add an on_step_begin callback (or use on_substep_end if available) that emits a synthetic progress event at regular intervals (e.g. every 30 seconds) even if the step hasn't completed. Alternatively, emit an initial progress event from on_train_begin with step=0, total_steps=max_steps, progress_pct=0, so the UI at least shows the total steps and knows training has started.

At minimum, on_train_begin should emit a progress event with total_steps so the UI can display 'step 0 / N' instead of just '0%'.

2. FRONTEND: The runs-page.tsx (line 193) only shows the ActiveRunBanner when selectedRunId === activeRun.id. If the user navigates to the page without clicking the active run in the list, no WS subscription is created (useRunStream gets runId=null), and the banner doesn't show. Auto-select the active run on page load.

Also: The useRuns query (REST) is not invalidated on progress_update events (useRunStream.ts lines 144-161), so even if DB is updated with progress, the REST-fetched run.progressPct stays stale until a stage change. Consider polling the active run at a low interval or invalidating on progress_update.

File area: backend/app/services/trainer.py (WorkbenchCallback), frontend/src/pages/runs-page.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useRunStream.ts
Acceptance: Progress bar shows step count and updates within 30 seconds of training start. Active run is auto-selected when navigating to Runs page.
closeReason: fixed progress bar stuck at 0%: emit initial progress event in on_train_begin, auto-select active run on page load, invalidate run query on progress_update events

title: training initialization and step progress should appear in logs view
description:
Currently the Logs tab on the Runs page shows nothing during training initialization and step execution. Users have no visibility into what the trainer is doing between stage transitions.

Expected behavior:
1. During model loading/initialization (model_resolution, dataset_resolution, tokenization, training_preparation stages), the trainer should emit info-level log lines describing what it is doing: 'Loading model from huggingface...', 'Tokenizing dataset (5176 rows)...', 'Preparing SFTConfig...', etc.
2. During training_progress, periodic log lines should appear showing step progress: 'Step 10/1294 (0.8%) — loss: 2.34, lr: 1.2e-4' (every logging_steps interval).
3. The on_log callback in WorkbenchCallback already emits metric events but does NOT emit a corresponding log line. Add a log emission in on_log so users see training progress in the Logs tab.
4. The on_step_end callback should emit a periodic log line (e.g. every 10 steps or every logging_steps) with current step/total and basic stats.

Backend changes needed:
- backend/app/services/trainer.py: Add _emit_log calls at key points in initialization stages (model load, dataset load, tokenization, trainer construction). Add log emission in WorkbenchCallback.on_log or on_step_end at logging_steps intervals.

Frontend: No changes needed — the log streaming infrastructure (useRunStream appendLogs, Logs tab) already works. Just needs the backend to emit the log events.

File area: backend/app/services/trainer.py
Acceptance: Logs tab shows descriptive messages during all initialization stages and periodic step progress during training.
closeReason: added training log emissions to trainer.py: descriptive _emit_log calls at dataset_resolution, tokenization_preprocessing, training_preparation stages, and step progress logging with loss/lr in WorkbenchCallback.on_log

title: improve watchdog failure diagnostics for terminated training processes
description: When a training subprocess dies unexpectedly (OOM, segfault, MPS crash), the watchdog marks it as 'Process terminated unexpectedly' with zero diagnostic detail. Improve recover_stale_runs and check_run_health to: (1) read last heartbeat metrics and include them in failure_reason, (2) check process exit signal via psutil or os.waitpid if available, (3) check macOS system log for OOM kills matching the PID, (4) include last known step/total_steps/stage in the failure message. Files: backend/app/services/watchdog.py, backend/app/core/config.py (if new settings needed). Do NOT modify orchestrator.py or trainer.py.

title: analyze run 6ca9b88f results and identify improvement areas
description: Analyze the completed run 6ca9b88f-fcfb-47e3-a6c2-7f58bd50a1c8 (project: test, 33 total steps, 100% complete). Review training metrics, loss curves, checkpoint quality, and config to identify areas of improvement. Run used config version 3ce88666-421d-4de0-800f-420ca1c14f52. Look at metrics_log entries, final loss values, learning rate schedule effectiveness, and whether the model converged or could benefit from more steps/different hyperparameters.

title: dynamic checkpoint and metrics logging interval based on total steps
description: Currently save_steps, logging_steps, and eval_steps are static defaults (100, 10, 50). Make these dynamic based on total training steps so they default to ~10% of total steps. Examples: 10000 steps → log/checkpoint every 1000; 100 steps → every 10; 33 steps → every 3. Implementation: in trainer.py _stage_training_preparation, after total_steps is known (from SFTTrainer or computed from dataset size/epochs/batch), compute dynamic intervals as max(1, total_steps // 10). Apply to save_steps, logging_steps, and eval_steps unless the user has explicitly overridden them in config. Key files: backend/app/services/trainer.py (lines 700-724 SFTConfig construction), backend/app/schemas/workbench_config.py (TrainingConfig defaults at lines 54-56), backend/app/services/project_service.py (default config generation at lines 234-236). The schema defaults should change to None (Optional[int]) to distinguish user-set from auto, with the dynamic calculation as fallback.

title: timeline shows evaluation and checkpoint_save as grey/incomplete when skipped
description: In run 6ca9b88f (33 total steps), stages 11 (evaluation) and 12 (checkpoint_save) show as grey unfilled circles in the timeline instead of a distinct 'skipped' or 'not applicable' state. This happened because eval_steps=100 and save_steps=100 exceeded total steps (33), so these stages never triggered. The timeline should visually distinguish between: (1) completed stages (green/blue), (2) skipped stages (e.g. greyed out with a skip indicator or dash), (3) failed stages (red), (4) pending/not-started stages. Investigate both backend stage tracking (does the run record these as skipped vs pending?) and frontend timeline rendering. Key areas: backend stage lifecycle in trainer.py and orchestrator, frontend run timeline component, and the stage status types.

title: openai settings: default model dropdown and simplify to just api key
description: In the settings page, the OpenAI connection should be simplified so the user only needs to provide their API key. The base URL should be pre-filled/hardcoded (https://api.openai.com/v1) since it's not something users should need to figure out. Add a dropdown of common OpenAI models (gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4-turbo, gpt-3.5-turbo, etc.) instead of requiring manual model name entry. The goal is to reduce friction — user pastes their key, picks a model from the dropdown, done. Investigate current settings UI and backend config for AI/LLM provider connections.

title: fix: no api key configured error when key is set correctly
description: After the OpenAI settings simplification, entering a valid API key and selecting a model still shows 'No API key configured' error. Likely the settings save/load path is broken — either the new 'openai' provider type isn't being persisted correctly, or the backend settings_service.py isn't reading the key from the right field. Investigate the full flow: frontend settings form submission -> backend save -> backend retrieval when AI features are used. Check settings_service.py for how it resolves the API key for the 'openai' provider type.

title: add claude model dropdown with correct model IDs
description: Add 'Anthropic' / 'Claude' as a distinct AI provider option in settings (like OpenAI now has), with a dropdown of available Claude models using their correct API model IDs. Use context7 MCP to look up the latest Anthropic API documentation for current model IDs. Base URL should be hardcoded to https://api.anthropic.com. User only needs to provide their API key and pick a model.

title: add o1, o1-mini, o3-mini to openai model dropdown
description: Follow-up from llm-interactive-cc91. The OPENAI_MODELS array in frontend/src/components/settings/settings-form.tsx needs o1, o1-mini, and o3-mini added to the dropdown list.

title: separate set button for api key instead of requiring save settings
description: Currently the API key is saved as part of the general 'Save Settings' action. Users expect a dedicated 'Set' or 'Save Key' button next to the API key input that immediately persists the key without having to save all settings. The API key field should have its own inline action button (e.g. 'Set Key') that calls the backend to persist just the key. Visual feedback (checkmark, toast) should confirm the key was saved. File area: frontend/src/components/settings/settings-form.tsx, backend/app/api/routes/ (settings routes), backend/app/services/settings_service.py.

title: fix 422 error on ai suggestion for run - wire up full request flow
description: When requesting an AI suggestion for a run, the frontend gets a 422 Unprocessable Entity from the backend. This means the request payload shape doesn't match what the backend route expects. Investigate the full flow: (1) frontend — what endpoint is called and what payload is sent when requesting AI suggestions for a run, (2) backend route — what Pydantic request schema does it expect, (3) the ai_recommender service — is it properly wired to handle all provider types (openai, anthropic, openai-compatible). Recent changes added 'openai' and 'anthropic' as distinct providers and fixed camelCase/snake_case key conversion in settings — the 422 may be related to the request body not matching the expected schema, or the route/service not being fully wired. Check backend logs for the validation error details. Key files: frontend/src/api/ (AI suggestion calls), backend/app/api/routes/ (AI routes), backend/app/schemas/ (request schemas), backend/app/services/ai_recommender.py.

title: fix 500 error on ai suggestion generate endpoint
description: POST /api/v1/projects/eb9af749-e4aa-414b-b865-28c38c0a4e58/suggestions/generate returns 500 Internal Server Error. The 422 was fixed but now the actual generation is failing server-side. Need to check backend logs for the traceback — likely an issue in suggestion_service.py or ai_recommender.py when actually calling the AI provider API. Could be: wrong API call format for the provider, missing/malformed headers (e.g. Anthropic requires different auth headers than OpenAI), base_url misconfiguration, or the provider routing logic not handling all cases. Check the full stack trace in the backend console output.

title: fix 500 on suggestion generate: base_url not resolved for openai/anthropic providers
description: build_engine() in suggestion_service.py passes current_settings.ai_base_url directly, which is None for 'openai' and 'anthropic' providers (base_url gets cleared on provider switch). CloudLLMEngine then passes None to the SDK client. Compare with test_ai_connection() in settings_service.py which correctly hardcodes 'https://api.openai.com/v1' for openai and omits base_url for anthropic. Fix: either (1) resolve the base_url in suggestion_service.py before calling build_engine (openai -> 'https://api.openai.com/v1', anthropic -> None/default), or (2) resolve it inside CloudLLMEngine.__init__ or _call_api based on provider. The openai Python SDK with base_url=None may work but the anthropic SDK should use its default. Also verify the provider value stored in settings matches what _call_api expects ('openai' vs 'openai_compatible'). Key files: backend/app/services/suggestion_service.py (line ~175), backend/app/services/ai_recommender.py (CloudLLMEngine), backend/app/services/settings_service.py (test_ai_connection as reference).

title: audit windows compatibility across frontend, backend, and docker
description: Comprehensive audit of Windows compatibility for the entire workbench. Check: (1) Backend Python — pathlib usage vs hardcoded '/' separators, os.fork/signal usage, subprocess spawning (MPS/CUDA device detection), file permissions, symlinks, atomic file operations (os.rename behavior on Windows), watchdog process monitoring (os.waitpid, signals). (2) Frontend — any OS-specific assumptions in dev server config, vite proxy, file path handling. (3) Docker — Dockerfile compatibility, volume mounts, line endings (CRLF vs LF). (4) Dependencies — check all pip and npm packages for Windows support (especially PyTorch, bitsandbytes, peft). (5) Scripts/tooling — any shell scripts, Makefile targets, or dev tooling that assumes Unix. (6) Data paths — sqlite path handling, checkpoint/project directory creation. Report findings with specific file:line references and severity (blocker vs cosmetic).

title: platform-aware process signals: SIGSTOP/SIGCONT + SIGTERM
description: orchestrator.py:841,872 uses SIGSTOP/SIGCONT for pause/resume, trainer.py:860-861 uses SIGTERM for graceful shutdown. These signals don't exist on Windows. Add platform detection: on macOS/Linux keep existing signal-based code unchanged, on Windows use psutil to suspend/resume processes and a cooperative cancellation mechanism (e.g. cancel flag file the trainer polls). Key files: backend/app/services/orchestrator.py, backend/app/services/trainer.py. IMPORTANT: do not modify existing macOS/Unix code paths — wrap them in platform checks and add Windows alternatives alongside.

title: platform-aware watchdog: os.waitpid and exit status parsing
description: watchdog.py:101 uses os.waitpid(pid, os.WNOHANG) which is Unix-only. os.WIFSIGNALED, os.WTERMSIG, os.WIFEXITED, os.WEXITSTATUS (watchdog.py:103-108) don't exist on Windows. Add platform detection: on macOS/Linux keep existing waitpid code unchanged, on Windows use psutil.Process(pid).status() / psutil.Process(pid).wait(timeout=0) for cross-platform process monitoring. Key file: backend/app/services/watchdog.py. IMPORTANT: do not modify existing macOS/Unix code paths.

title: platform-aware health endpoint: disk usage and misc fixes
description: health.py:22 uses shutil.disk_usage('/') which is invalid on Windows. trainer.py:215 uses Path.rename() which raises FileExistsError on Windows if target exists. Add platform detection: (1) health.py — on Windows use shutil.disk_usage('.') or detect the drive letter, on macOS/Unix keep '/'. (2) trainer.py atomic checkpoint writes — on Windows use shutil.move() or pre-delete target, on macOS/Unix keep existing rename. (3) Add .gitattributes line ending rules (* text=auto, *.py text eol=lf). (4) Add .dockerignore with standard exclusions. Key files: backend/app/api/routes/health.py, backend/app/services/trainer.py, .gitattributes, .dockerignore. IMPORTANT: do not modify existing macOS/Unix code paths — add Windows alternatives alongside.

title: make bitsandbytes optional for Windows compatibility
description: bitsandbytes>=0.43.0 in pyproject.toml has poor Windows support. Make it an optional dependency so the app still starts on Windows without it. QLoRA/4-bit quantization should gracefully degrade when bitsandbytes is unavailable — detect at runtime, show a clear message if the user tries to use QLoRA without it. Key files: pyproject.toml, backend/app/services/trainer.py (where quantization config is applied), backend/app/adapters/ (if bitsandbytes is imported there). IMPORTANT: on macOS/Linux where bitsandbytes is installed, behavior must be identical to current.

title: code review: windows compatibility implementation
description: Review all Windows compatibility changes merged in this session. Verify: (1) All platform checks use consistent pattern (platform.system() == 'Windows' vs _IS_UNIX etc). (2) No existing macOS/Unix code paths were accidentally modified. (3) Windows code paths are correct — psutil suspend/resume in orchestrator.py, psutil process monitoring in watchdog.py, disk_usage with correct drive in health.py, shutil.move for atomic rename in trainer.py, cancel flag file mechanism in trainer.py. (4) bitsandbytes optional dependency is properly guarded with try/except and clear error messages. (5) .gitattributes and .dockerignore are correct. (6) No type errors, unused imports, or dead code introduced. (7) All error handling follows project conventions (no bare except, typed exceptions). Files to review: backend/app/services/orchestrator.py, backend/app/services/trainer.py, backend/app/services/watchdog.py, backend/app/api/routes/health.py, backend/pyproject.toml, .gitattributes, .dockerignore.
closeReason: Review passed, platform detection fix merged

(1) Add environment field to ExecutionConfig in backend/app/schemas/workbench_config.py: environment: Literal['local', 'modal'] = 'local', modal_gpu_type: Literal['t4', 'a10', 'a100-40gb', 'a100-80gb', 'h100'] | None = None. (4) Update frontend types in frontend/src/types/config.ts to include environment and modal_gpu_type. File area: backend/app/schemas/workbench_config.py, backend/app/services/settings_service.py, backend/app/api/routes/settings.py, backend/app/schemas/settings.py, frontend/src/types/config.ts, frontend/src/types/cloud.ts (new).

title: modal integration: training dispatcher abstraction + orchestrator wiring
description: Create the dispatcher service that sits between the orchestrator and execution. (1) New file backend/app/services/training_dispatcher.py — defines TrainingProcess abstract handle (for event streaming + cancellation) and dispatch_training() function that checks environment config: 'local' -> spawns subprocess as today, 'modal' -> delegates to ModalTrainingAdapter. (2) Modify backend/app/services/orchestrator.py — replace direct subprocess spawning in _run_trainer_subprocess with a call to the dispatcher. The dispatcher returns a TrainingProcess handle. All event processing (_process_trainer_event), DB writes, and WebSocket broadcasting remain completely unchanged. (3) Update backend/app/api/routes/runs.py to pass environment config through to orchestrator. IMPORTANT: the local code path through the dispatcher must produce identical behavior to today — zero regression. File area: backend/app/services/training_dispatcher.py (new), backend/app/services/orchestrator.py, backend/app/api/routes/runs.py. Depends on: schema issue (ExecutionConfig with environment field).

title: modal integration: modal training adapter
description: Implement the Modal cloud training adapter. New files: backend/app/services/cloud/__init__.py, backend/app/services/cloud/modal_adapter.py. The ModalTrainingAdapter implements the TrainingProcess interface from the dispatcher. Responsibilities: (1) Job submission — packages trainer code + config into a Modal function call with specified GPU type. trainer.py 14-stage pipeline runs inside Modal container. (2) Event relay — captures JSON-lines stdout from remote Modal process and feeds events back through the same event stream the orchestrator already consumes. (3) Checkpoint handling — training writes checkpoints to a Modal Volume, adapter downloads checkpoint artifacts to local project storage on completion. (4) Cancellation — maps orchestrator cancel request to Modal job cancellation API. (5) Heartbeat synthesis — polls Modal job status periodically and synthesizes heartbeat updates for watchdog compatibility. (6) Dataset upload — uploads training dataset to Modal Volume before job start. Pin modal SDK version in pyproject.toml. File area: backend/app/services/cloud/__init__.py (new), backend/app/services/cloud/modal_adapter.py (new), backend/pyproject.toml (add modal dep). Depends on: dispatcher issue (TrainingProcess interface).

title: modal integration: frontend UI — settings, environment selector, GPU picker, run badges
description: All frontend changes for Modal cloud training.
